title: Oxopiperidine quantitation by mass spectrometry
abstract: Methods for determining the presence or amount of oxopiperidine in a biological sample using mass spectrometry. These methods may be used to efficiently and non-invasively diagnose pyridoxine dependent epilepsy (PDE) due to deficient a-aminoadipic-Î´- semialdehyde (Î±-AASA) dehydrogenase activity due to mutations in ALDH7A1 , resulting in the accumulation of Î 1 -Î¡6C, P6CH, and 6-Oxo-PIP in biological samples..
claims:
Claims What is Claimed is: 1. A method for detection of 6-oxopiperidine-2-carboxylic acid (Oxo-PIP) in a sample, comprising: a) introducing an internal standard into a sample received from a patient; and, b) analyzing the sample containing the internal standard using liquid chromatography tandem mass spectrometer (LC-MS-MS) to determine the Oxo- PIP concentration present in the sample. 2. The method of claim 1 further comprising: adding at least one of an alcohol and an organic solvent to the sample containing the internal standard prior to the analyzing step b). 3. The method of claim 2, further comprising: mixing the sample containing the internal standard after addition of the at least one of an alcohol and an organic solvent to form a mixture; centrifuging the mixture to form a supernatant in the mixture; recovering at least a portion of the supernatant and applying the supernatant to the LC in analyzing step b). 4. The method of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the internal standard is a deuterated internal standard. 5. The method of claim 4 wherein the internal standard is deuterated Oxo-PIP (D 3 -Oxo- PIP). 6. The method of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the sample is a biological fluid selected from saliva, sweat, urine, blood, serum, plasma, cerebrospinal fluid (CSF), and combinations thereof. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the transitions for Oxo-PIP in mass spectra with tandem mass spectrometer are at 144.2 to 98.1. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the liquid chromatography tandem mass spectrometer (LC-MS-MS) comprises electrospray ionization (ESI) MS. 9. A method of diagnosing or monitoring pyridoxine dependent epilepsy (PDE) comprising the steps of claim 1, wherein the presence of Oxo-PIP in the sample is diagnostic of PDE or progression of PDE in a patient that provided the sample. 10. A method of detecting Oxo-PIP in a patient, comprising: a) obtaining a sample from a patient; and b) detecting whether Oxo-PIP is present in the sample by contacting the sample with 

 an internal standard and detecting the presence or concentration of Oxo-PIP by liquid chromatography tandem mass spectrometer (LC-MS-MS). 11. The method of claim 10 further comprising: adding at least one of an alcohol and an organic solvent to the sample containing the internal standard prior to the analyzing step b). 12. The method of claim 11, further comprising: mixing the sample containing the internal standard after addition of the at least one of an alcohol and an organic solvent to form a mixture; centrifuging the mixture to form a supernatant in the mixture; recovering at least a portion of the supernatant and applying the supernatant to the LC in analyzing step b). 13. The method of any one of claims 10-12, wherein the internal standard is a deuterated internal standard. 14. The method of claim 13 wherein the internal standard is deuterated Oxo-PIP (D 3 - Oxo-PIP). 15. The method of any one of claims 10-14, wherein the sample is a biological fluid selected from saliva, sweat, urine, blood, serum, plasma, cerebrospinal fluid (CSF), and combinations thereof. 16. The method of any one of claims 10-15, wherein the transitions for Oxo-PIP in mass spectra with tandem mass spectrometer are at 144.2 to 98.1. 17. The method of any one of claims 10-16, wherein the liquid chromatography tandem mass spectrometer (LC-MS-MS) comprises electrospray ionization (ESI) MS. 18. The method of any one of claims 10-17, wherein the patient is a human patient. 19. A method of diagnosing pyridoxine dependent epilepsy (PDE) in a patient, comprising: a) obtaining a sample from a human patient; b) detecting whether Oxo-PIP is present in the sample by contacting the sample with an internal standard and detecting the presence or concentration of Oxo-PIP by liquid chromatography tandem mass spectrometer (LC-MS-MS); and c) diagnosing the patient with PDE when the presence of Oxo-PIP is detected in the sample. 20. The method of claim 19 further comprising: adding at least one of an alcohol and an organic solvent to the sample containing the 

 internal standard prior to the analyzing step b). 21. The method of claim 20, further comprising: mixing the sample containing the internal standard after addition of the at least one of an alcohol and an organic solvent to form a mixture; centrifuging the mixture to form a supernatant in the mixture; recovering at least a portion of the supernatant and applying the supernatant to the LC in analyzing step b). 22. The method of any one of claims 19-21, wherein the internal standard is a deuterated internal standard. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the internal standard is deuterated Oxo-PIP (D 3 - Oxo-PIP). 24. The method of any one of claims 19-23, wherein the sample is a biological fluid selected from saliva, sweat, urine, blood, serum, plasma, cerebrospinal fluid (CSF), and combinations thereof. 25. The method of any one of claims 19-24, wherein the transitions for Oxo-PIP in mass spectra with tandem mass spectrometer are at 144.2 to 98.1. 26. The method of any one of claims 19-25, wherein the liquid chromatography tandem mass spectrometer (LC-MS-MS) comprises electrospray ionization (ESI) MS. 27. A method of diagnosing pyridoxine dependent epilepsy (PDE) in a patient, comprising: a) obtaining a sample from a human patient; b) detecting whether Oxo-PIP is present in the sample; and c) diagnosing the patient with PDE when the presence of Oxo-PIP in the sample is detected. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the sample is a biological fluid selected from saliva, sweat, urine, blood, serum, plasma, cerebrospinal fluid (CSF), and combinations thereof. 29. A method of diagnosing and treating PDE in a patient, comprising: a) obtaining a sample from a human patient; b) detecting whether Oxo-PIP is present in the sample; c) diagnosing the patient with PDE when the presence of Oxo-PIP in the sample is detected; and, d) administering an effective amount of vitamin B6 to the diagnosed patient. 30. The method of claim 29 further comprising: adding at least one of an alcohol and an organic solvent to the sample containing the internal standard prior to the analyzing step b). 31. The method of claim 30, further comprising: mixing the sample containing the internal standard after addition of the at least one of an alcohol and an organic solvent to form a mixture; centrifuging the mixture to form a supernatant in the mixture; recovering at least a portion of the supernatant and applying the supernatant to the LC in analyzing step b). 32. The method of any one of claims 29-31, wherein the internal standard is a deuterated internal standard. 33. The method of claim 32 wherein the internal standard is deuterated Oxo-PIP (D 3 - Oxo-PIP). 34. The method of any one of claims 29-33, wherein the sample is a biological fluid selected from saliva, sweat, urine, blood, serum, plasma, cerebrospinal fluid (CSF), and combinations thereof. 35. The method of any one of claims 29-34, wherein the transitions for Oxo-PIP in mass spectra with tandem mass spectrometer are at 144.2 to 98.1. 36. The method of any one of claims 29-35, wherein the liquid chromatography tandem mass spectrometer (LC-MS-MS) comprises electrospray ionization (ESI) MS. 37. The method of any one of claims 29-36, wherein the patient is a human patient. 38. A method of monitoring the presence or progression of PDE in a patient, comprising: a) obtaining a sample from a human patient; b) detecting the concentration of Oxo-PIP in the sample; c) administering a therapeutic change to a PDE treatment in the patient if the concentration of Oxo-PIP is detected in the sample is greater than 100pmol/mg creatinine. 39. The method of claim 38, further comprising: adding at least one of an alcohol and an organic solvent to the sample containing the internal standard prior to the analyzing step b). 40. The method of claim 39, further comprising: mixing the sample containing the internal standard after addition of the at least one 

 of an alcohol and an organic solvent to form a mixture; centrifuging the mixture to form a supernatant in the mixture; recovering at least a portion of the supernatant and applying the supernatant to the LC in analyzing step b). 41 The method of any one of claims 38-40, wherein the internal standard is a deuterated internal standard. 42. The method of claim 41 wherein the internal standard is deuterated Oxo-PIP (D 3 - Oxo-PIP). 43. The method of any one of claims 38-41, wherein the sample is a biological fluid selected from saliva, sweat, urine, blood, serum, plasma, cerebrospinal fluid (CSF), and combinations thereof. 44. The method of any one of claims 38-43, wherein the transitions for Oxo-PIP in mass spectra with tandem mass spectrometer are at 144.2 to 98.1. 45. The method of any one of claims 38-44, wherein the liquid chromatography tandem mass spectrometer (LC-MS-MS) comprises electrospray ionization (ESI) MS. 46. The method of any one of claims 38-45, wherein the therapeutic change administered to the patient comprises an administration of Vitamin B6. 47. The method of any one of claims 38-45, wherein the therapeutic change administered to the patient comprises dietary restriction of lysine in the patientâs diet. 48. A deuterated compound selected from the group consisting of 2-Amino-l,6-hexanedioic-2,5,5-d3 Acid (D3-AAA), and, deuterated 6-oxopiperidine-2-carboxylic acid. 49. A deuterated compound comprising a chemical structure selected from the group consisting of: 50. A method of making a deuterated oxopiperidine comprising: a) contacting 2-Amino-l,6-hexanedioic-2,5,5-d3 Acid (D3-AAA) with mono- deuteroacetic acid (CH 3 C00D)/D20 to form a solution; b) adding deuterated ethanol (CH3CH2OD) to the solution; c) recovering deuterated Oxo-PIP from the solution as a solid filtrate. 51. A method of making a deuterated oxopiperidine comprising: a) refluxing D3-AAA in 20% mono-deuteroacetic acid (CH 3 C00D)/D20 at 108 Â°C for 3.5 hours to form a solution; b) evaporating solvent under reduced pressure from the solution; c) drying the solution on high vacuum for about 1.0 hour to form a reaction mixture; d) adding deuterated ethanol (CH3CH2OD; 10 mL) to the reaction mixture; e) filtering the reaction mixture through filter paper to recover a solid filtrate; f) adding additional deuterated ethanol to the solid filtrate and mixing the suspension; g) filtering the suspension through filter paper to collect a second filtrate; h) concentrating the second filtrate on reduced pressure and drying the second filtrate on high vacuum to recover deuterated Oxo-PIP at >97% purity.
